She doesn't wait for an answer to that, her focus shifting. ***"You say you 'broke it.' What was required to break an artifact powerful enough to create a void in the Force?"***

Ciara gives an easy smile, her fatigue fading now that she's got some distance from the ward. She takes a long moment to consider the questions, wondering what Sarya is thinking. She has some guesses, and isn't concerned with the specifics. At least from here, she won't feel any disappointment, and Sarya is kind enough to keep those feelings from spilling out. 

Ciara's smile softens. "I'll address your first question first. On another day, with different circumstances, I might have supported such an idea. If he hadn't gotten the jedi involved, if he was only using criminals who were destined to die anyway, or perhaps volunteers... I can see myself overlooking the force wound, focusing on the results." She meets Sarya's eyes, without an ounce of doubt within her own. "Today though, what needed to be done was clear. In anyone's hands, this artifact would have caused disaster. It had to be destroyed." 

She breaks eye contact and moves into a full body stretch. "As for the artifact itself, breaking it was fairly easy." As the stretch concludes her smile twists into a grin, almost a touch of mischief in the expression. She gives a light shrug, "Kaelan just hit it with a lightsaber and that was that." Then, cocking her head to the side, she adds "I did squeeze it a bit, with the force." She makes a motion with both hands as if compressing something invisible.  "I was mostly trying to keep it's effects from spreading, but you know," she compresses the hands a little more, then stops, letting her arms fall to her sides. "forcefully."

Sarya's holographic form remains still, her expression softening with a deep, knowing empathy that even the flickering blue image cannot hide. She lets out a slow, quiet breath.

***"That honesty, Ciara... that is why I have always had faith in you. The desire to end suffering is the heart of a Jedi. It is not a flaw."*** Her voice is gentle, but firm. ***"But the moment we decide whose life is a resource... whose pain is a cost... we become the very thing we fight against. You saw that line today, and you chose not to cross it. That is a greater victory than you know."***

A small, wry smile touches her lips. ***"'Squeezed it a bit,' she says. Ciara, to overpower an artifact like that, even a fractured one... that required a staggering amount of your will. Do not be so casual about it. I can see the toll it has taken on you even from here."***

The smile fades, replaced by a deep-seated seriousness. ***"A recovery and containment team is already on its way to Gareth Station. They will secure the fragments and take Dr. Thorne into Jedi custody for healing and judgment. Your mission there is complete."***

She pauses, her gaze becoming distant and troubled. ***"But this event has raised a new concern. The artifact. It feeds on the echoes of the Great Sith War. The psychic wounds of the Mandalorian War have not healed. They are festering in the Force, poisoning places and people."***

***"The Council agrees. They want you and Kaelan to return to the Temple at once. We need to understand the nature of this 'emotional echo' you are so uniquely attuned to. Your next mission is already waiting."***

Ciara isn't so sure that it's as clear as Sarya makes it sound. The Jedi already have to choose that some lives are an acceptable loss during this civil war. However, it *is* clear this time, so she doesn't feel the need to start a debate over the holocall.

Ciara truly can't be sure that her efforts made the difference with the artifact, it was the strike from Kaelan that shattered it, It's possible it would have done so without her help. Still, she does know that her effort yielded some sort of result. It isn't important which of them contributed more to its destruction, so she doesn't consider it further. 

Ciara nods to Sarya. "I'll collect Kaelan and we will be on our way as soon as possible." 

She leaves a notable pause, separating her official report from her next line. She realizes that if she didn't always conduct herself like she were having a friendly chat, she could signal a change here with body language and tone. 

"How have things been, at the temple?"

The hologram of Master Sarya flickers for a moment, and Ciara gets the distinct sense that her simple, personal question has landed with unexpected weight. When Sarya's image stabilizes, the serene mask of the Jedi Master has slipped just a fraction. Ciara sees her mentor, and she looks tired.

***"The Temple... is a place of ghosts, Ciara,"*** Sarya says, her warm voice now tinged with a deep weariness. ***"The war has taken so many. The schism with Revan's followers has left a wound in the Order that will not heal. We are fractured. There are whispers of a 'Sith Empire,' but the Council is paralyzed by caution, haunted by the fear of another Exar Kun. They forbid any from seeking answers."***

She lets out a soft sigh, a sound heavy with unspoken frustrations. ***"Masters argue in shadows, Knights grow restless, and the halls are too quiet. We are needed in the galaxy, but we spend our days debating what it means to be a Jedi while the Republic burns."***

Her expression shifts, the Jedi Master returning. ***"But that is a burden for the Council to carry. Your path is now clear. Return to us. The insight you have gained into these... psychic wounds... may be the key to helping us find our way again."***

Ciara wonders silently what she would have chose if she had been a Jedi when Revan called to fight against the mandalorians. It doesn't take long for her to know the answer. She would have followed Revan, would have felt that it was wrong to deny the republic the help it needed in the conflict. She would see joining the war as the best, not to mention the quickest, way to end the war. 

She then wonders if she would have continued following Revan beyond the reaches of known space, then returned to attack those she once called allies. This one is difficult to imagine. Perhaps if she saw whatever Revan saw beyond the outer rim, she would agree with him. She wants to imagine that she would have returned to the Jedi as soon as the Mandalorian wars ended... but she can't be sure. 

Knowing that they are needed now but that they spend time debating while the galaxy burns... This story echoes the prelude to Revan joining the Mandalorian wars. If someone like him were to rise now and prioritize action again- She dismisses the thought, now isn't the time to consider it. 

A slight frown forms on her face as Sarya concludes the conversation once more. It's quickly replaced with her standard easygoing smile. "I'll share whatever information I can. See you soon, Master."
